meta.name: Spain
meta.aboutStudying: Spain offers world-class public universities at very affordable tuition — typically €700–2,500/year. With a vibrant culture, Mediterranean lifestyle and one of the largest Erasmus communities in Europe, it's a top destination for international students.

tips.title: Visa Requirements
tips.text: EU citizens enter freely. Non-EU students need a student visa — apply at least 3 months before your programme starts at the nearest Spanish consulate.

tips.title: Cost of Living
tips.text: Budget €600–€1,600/month depending on city. Salamanca and Granada are cheapest; Madrid and Barcelona the priciest. Student discounts are widespread.

tips.title: Language Options
tips.text: Most public universities teach in Spanish. Many private schools and some public ones offer English-taught degrees — check each university's language offerings.

universities.name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
universities.desc: Spain's largest and one of the world's oldest universities, with a vast campus covering virtually every discipline.

universities.name: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid
universities.desc: Research-intensive autonomous university renowned for excellence in science, medicine and humanities.

universities.name: Universidad Politécnica de Madrid
universities.desc: Spain's leading technical university with top-ranked engineering, architecture and computer science programmes.

universities.name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
universities.desc: Modern public university highly regarded for law, business and engineering, with strong English-taught and bilingual programmes.

universities.name: IE University
universities.desc: Elite private institution with globally ranked business and law degrees, delivered entirely in English or bilingually.

universities.name: Universidad Rey Juan Carlos
universities.desc: Large public university in the Madrid metropolitan area with accessible entry requirements covering law, communication and engineering.

universities.name: Universidad de Barcelona
universities.desc: Catalonia's flagship university with a long history and a wide range of programmes in the vibrant heart of Barcelona.

universities.name: Universidad Autónoma de Barcelona
universities.desc: Research powerhouse near Barcelona known for sciences, medicine and social sciences, with a strong international profile.

universities.name: Universidad Pompeu Fabra
universities.desc: Compact elite university consistently ranked among Spain's best for law, economics and journalism in central Barcelona.

universities.name: Universidad Politécnica de Cataluña
universities.desc: Catalonia's top technical university for engineering, architecture and CS, with strong links to Barcelona's tech industry.

universities.name: ESADE Business School
universities.desc: World-renowned private school ranked among Europe's top MBA programmes, with a focus on responsible leadership.

universities.name: Universidad de Valencia
universities.desc: One of Spain's oldest universities offering a broad curriculum in a vibrant Mediterranean city with low living costs.

universities.name: Universidad Politécnica de Valencia
universities.desc: Top technical university in Valencia with excellent engineering, architecture and informatics departments.

universities.name: Universidad de Sevilla
universities.desc: Andalusia's largest and most prestigious university, rich in history and offering extensive programmes in medicine, law and arts.

universities.name: Universidad de Granada
universities.desc: Historic university in one of Spain's most beautiful student cities, known for humanities, sciences and its welcoming atmosphere.

universities.name: Universidad de Salamanca
universities.desc: One of Europe's oldest universities and a living monument to scholarship, offering law, medicine and humanities at very low cost.

universities.name: Universidad del País Vasco (EHU)
universities.desc: The main public university of the Basque Country, renowned for bilingual education in Spanish and Basque and strong research output.

universities.name: Universidad de Deusto
universities.desc: Prestigious Jesuit private university known for law, business and social sciences, with English-taught options and low-barrier entry.

universities.name: Universidad de Zaragoza
universities.desc: Aragon's flagship university with five centuries of history — respected throughout Spain for engineering, sciences and medicine at very affordable costs.

universities.name: Universidad de Valladolid
universities.desc: One of Spain's oldest universities — strong in engineering and science in one of Spain's most architecturally rich cities.

universities.name: Universidad de Málaga
universities.desc: A growing modern university on the Costa del Sol — increasingly recognised for CS and engineering in Spain's fastest-growing tech hub.

universities.name: Universidad de Alicante
universities.desc: Set on Spain's sunny Costa Blanca — one of Spain's most internationally diverse universities, popular with Erasmus students for its beach lifestyle and low costs.

universities.name: Universidad de Murcia
universities.desc: One of Spain's oldest public universities, strong in medicine, law and tourism studies in the warm agricultural city of Murcia.

universities.name: Universidad de Oviedo
universities.desc: Asturias's public university with strengths in medicine, mining engineering and geology in the charming pre-Romanesque capital — gateway to Spain's green north.

universities.name: Universidad de Santiago de Compostela
universities.desc: One of the world's most symbolic university cities — the Camino de Santiago ends at its cathedral. Strong in medicine and humanities with a magnificent Baroque campus.

universities.name: Universidad de La Laguna
universities.desc: Spain's oldest colonial university on Tenerife — near Mount Teide and world-class astrophysics observatories in an eternal spring climate on the Canary Islands.

universities.name: Universidad de Cádiz
universities.desc: In one of Europe's oldest cities on the Atlantic coast — affordable and relaxed, with a strong focus on maritime engineering and marine biology.

universities.name: Universidad de Córdoba
universities.desc: In the stunning UNESCO World Heritage city of Córdoba — compact and affordable, with strengths in agriculture, veterinary science and environmental engineering.

universities.name: Universidad de Extremadura
universities.desc: Spain's most affordable university region — dual-campus across Badajoz and Cáceres, offering nursing, agriculture and fine arts at some of Spain's lowest fees.

universities.name: Universidad de Castilla-La Mancha
universities.desc: A multi-campus university spread across Don Quixote's La Mancha region with campuses in Toledo, Albacete, Cuenca and Ciudad Real.

universities.name: Universidad de Cantabria
universities.desc: Compact public university in the elegant Cantabrian capital of Santander — known for civil and industrial engineering, physics and an outdoor mountain-and-sea campus setting.

universities.name: Universitat Rovira i Virgili
universities.desc: Catalonia's youngest public university in the Roman city of Tarragona — strong in chemical engineering, nutrition and oenology (wine science) in the Priorat wine country.

universities.name: Universitat de Girona
universities.desc: In one of Catalonia's most beautiful medieval cities — Girona is internationally oriented, attracting students for tourism management and environmental science.

universities.name: Universitat Jaume I
universities.desc: A young, forward-thinking university on Valencia's northern coast — particularly strong in translation studies, CS and chemical engineering in a friendly, compact city.

universities.name: Universidad de Navarra
universities.desc: Spain's most prestigious private university — consistently ranked #1 in Spain for medicine and law, with IESE Business School world-ranked for MBA programmes.

universities.name: Universidad Pontificia Comillas
universities.desc: Elite Jesuit university in Madrid — the ICAI School of Engineering and ICADE Business & Law School are among the most prestigious and employer-valued in Spain.

universities.name: Universidad CEU San Pablo
universities.desc: One of Madrid's leading private universities — highly regarded for medicine, architecture and journalism with a strong alumni network across Spanish business and public life.

universities.name: Universidad de A Coruña
universities.desc: Galicia's coastal public university — strong in naval and civil engineering, architecture and maritime studies in a charming Atlantic city.

universities.name: Universidad de Vigo
universities.desc: In Galicia's economic capital and Spain's largest fishing port — strong in marine biology, fisheries engineering and telecoms, and an ideal base for working across Portugal.

universities.name: Universitat de les Illes Balears
universities.desc: Set in Palma, Mallorca — the only public university in the Balearic Islands, known for tourism management and environmental science in a Mediterranean island setting.

universities.name: Universidad de Jaén
universities.desc: Amid the world's largest olive grove in the UNESCO landscape of Jaén province — strong in environmental engineering and olive oil technology, among the cheapest universities in Andalusia.

universities.name: Universidad Francisco de Vitoria
universities.desc: A modern Catholic private university on the outskirts of Madrid — growing quickly with English-taught degrees and strong networks in medicine, law and communication.

universities.name: Universidad de La Rioja
universities.desc: Spain's wine capital's own university — compact and affordable, with a surprising depth in food technology, oenology and engineering in the beautiful Rioja wine region.

universities.name: Universidad Pablo de Olavide
universities.desc: Andalusia's newest public university on the outskirts of Seville — innovative and compact, strong in social sciences and environmental studies.

universities.name: UNED (National Distance Education University)
universities.desc: Spain's largest university and Europe's second-largest, offering fully online degrees across all disciplines at the lowest tuition in Spain.

universities.name: Universitat de Lleida
universities.desc: One of Catalonia's oldest institutions reborn in 1991 — strong in agri-food science, veterinary medicine and teacher training in the fertile Lleida plains.

universities.name: Universidad Miguel Hernández
universities.desc: A young, agile public university near the coast — notably strong in optics, biomedical engineering and fine arts in the sunny province of Alicante.

universities.name: Universitat de Vic – UCC
universities.desc: A small Catalan private university known for health sciences, teacher education and an intimate campus environment near Vic.

universities.name: IESE Business School
universities.desc: Consistently ranked among the world's top 5 MBA programmes — IESE's case-method approach and alumni network span every continent.

universities.name: ESIC Business & Marketing School
universities.desc: Spain's leading specialist for business, marketing and e-commerce — popular with students seeking practical industry skills and strong placement networks.

universities.name: Universidad de Alcalá
universities.desc: One of Europe's oldest universities in the UNESCO World Heritage city of Alcalá — Cervantes' birthplace, with strong medicine and pharmacy programmes.

universities.name: Universidad Rey Juan Carlos II
universities.desc: One of Madrid's largest public universities — known for communication, film and media degrees alongside business and law in a multi-campus setup.

universities.name: Universidad Nebrija
universities.desc: A modern private university in Madrid renowned for its aviation school, linguistics programmes and strong English-language business degrees.

universities.name: Universidad Católica de Murcia
universities.desc: A growing Catholic private university strong in sports science, physiotherapy and medicine — popular with student-athletes from across Europe.

universities.name: Universidad Internacional de la Rioja
universities.desc: Spain's leading fully-online private university — flexible programmes designed for working professionals, with competitive fees and strong digital learning infrastructure.

universities.name: EAE Business School
universities.desc: A prestigious private business school in Madrid and Barcelona delivering practical MBA and master's programmes with strong industry partnerships.

universities.name: Universidad Alfonso X El Sabio
universities.desc: A private Madrid university named after Spain's scholar-king — known for medicine, architecture and engineering in a leafy campus.

universities.name: Universitat Ramon Llull
universities.desc: Barcelona's major private university federation — home to ESADE, La Salle and Blanquerna, offering elite business, law and communication programmes.

universities.name: Universidad Pontificia de Salamanca
universities.desc: One of Spain's oldest ecclesiastical universities — a living piece of medieval Salamanca offering theology, communication, law and psychology.

universities.name: ESNE – Universidad de Diseño
universities.desc: Spain's leading design and creativity university — top-ranked for animation, graphic design, UX/UI and fashion design, with campuses in Madrid and Barcelona.

universities.name: EU Business School
universities.desc: An international English-only private business school with campuses in Barcelona, Madrid, Geneva and Munich — popular with international students.

universities.name: Universidad Internacional de Andalucía
universities.desc: Andalusia's public international university — specialising in postgraduate and continuing education across four Andalusian campuses with an emphasis on innovation.

universities.name: Universidad de Las Palmas de Gran Canaria
universities.desc: The Canary Islands' Atlantic university — known for marine sciences, tourism management and engineering in an island setting with year-round sunshine.

universities.name: Universidad CEU Cardenal Herrera
universities.desc: The CEU group's Valencia campus — particularly strong in health sciences with a renowned dentistry school and competitive medicine programme.

universities.name: Universitat Internacional de Catalunya
universities.desc: A small private Catalan university delivering premium medicine, architecture and business programmes in a modern Barcelona campus.

universities.name: Universitat Abat Oliba CEU
universities.desc: A small Catholic private university in Barcelona known for its close-knit community and strong placement rates in law and communication.

universities.name: Universidad de Mondragón
universities.desc: A cooperative university rooted in the Basque cooperative movement — internationally recognised for its participatory business model and innovative engineering programmes.

universities.name: Universitat de les Illes Balears 2
universities.desc: The Balearic Islands' main public university — strong in tourism, environmental science and teacher training in a Mediterranean island paradise.

universities.name: IE Law School
universities.desc: IE University's elite law school — offering LLM and JD programmes entirely in English, with an alumni network spanning international law firms on six continents.

universities.name: Universidad Pública de Navarra
universities.desc: Navarra's public university — practical and modern, with strong links to the regional agri-food industry and a growing CS and engineering faculty.

universities.name: Universidad de Almería
universities.desc: Andalusia's sunniest university city on the Mediterranean coast — strong in agriculture, biotechnology and environmental engineering with very affordable fees.

universities.name: Universidad de Huelva
universities.desc: Andalusia's Atlantic coast university — strong in mining, energy and environmental sciences near the Doñana National Park.

universities.name: Universidad de Burgos
universities.desc: A young public university in the Camino de Santiago city of Burgos — strong in food technology, engineering and economics with affordable living costs.

universities.name: Universidad de Alcalá 2
universities.desc: Castile-La Mancha's multi-campus university in Toledo — a UNESCO World Heritage city, offering an eclectic mix of humanities, tourism and engineering.

universities.name: Universitat Oberta de Catalunya
universities.desc: Catalonia's fully-online public university — the most internationally recognised distance-learning institution in the Spanish-speaking world, with an entirely digital campus.

universities.name: IQS Universitat Ramon Llull
universities.desc: A specialist Jesuit technical university for chemistry, engineering and management — IQS graduates are among Spain's most sought-after by multinational companies.

universities.name: Blanquerna – Universitat Ramon Llull
universities.desc: Barcelona's leading private school for communication, journalism and health sciences — Blanquerna graduates are the backbone of Catalan media.

universities.name: Escola Superior de Disseny – ESDi
universities.desc: Barcelona's specialist design university — top-ranked for industrial design, interior design and digital communication in the creative capital of southern Europe.

universities.name: Universidad San Jorge
universities.desc: Aragon's private university focused on communication sciences, health sciences and architecture in a modern campus in Zaragoza.

universities.name: Universidad Europea de Madrid
universities.desc: One of Madrid's largest private universities — offering medicine, engineering and business with a strong sports-and-health focus and English bilingual tracks.

universities.name: Universidad Francisco de Vitoria 2
universities.desc: A humanist private university that integrates ethics and leadership into all degree programmes — popular with students seeking a values-driven education.

universities.name: CUNEF Universidad
universities.desc: Madrid's elite private school for business and law — consistently producing the most sought-after graduates in Spanish investment banking and commercial law.

universities.name: Universidad de Castilla-La Mancha (Ciudad Real)
universities.desc: One of UCLM's main campuses in Don Quijote country — practical engineering, environmental science and social work degrees at Spain's most affordable tuition.

universities.name: Universitat de Barcelona – IL3
universities.desc: UB's lifelong learning institute offering continuing education, master's programmes and professional certificates for working adults.

universities.name: ELISAVA – Escola Superior de Disseny
universities.desc: Barcelona's oldest and most prestigious design and engineering school — known for product design, interaction design and collaborative creative projects.

universities.name: BAU – Escola Superior de Disseny
universities.desc: A creative private design school in Barcelona offering degrees in graphic design, art direction and fashion — internationally recognised for its vibrant student projects.

universities.name: ESERP Business & Law School
universities.desc: A practical private school for business and law in Barcelona and Madrid — known for its hands-on curriculum and strong graduate placement rates.

universities.name: Universidad de Navarra – Tecnun
universities.desc: The engineering campus of the University of Navarra — consistently ranked Spain's best engineering school, particularly strong in industrial and biomedical engineering.

universities.name: Centro Universitario de Tecnología y Arte Digital – U-tad
universities.desc: Spain's most specialised school for video game development, animation and digital arts — producing graduates who work for EA, Ubisoft and Netflix.

universities.name: Universidad de Deusto – San Sebastián
universities.desc: Deusto's San Sebastián campus in the heart of the Basque gourmet capital — known for law, psychology and tourism management.

universities.name: Universidad de Huelva – Campus del Carmen
universities.desc: Huelva's second campus specialising in maritime engineering, primary education and social work on Spain's less-visited Atlantic coast.

universities.name: Universidad de Málaga – Campus Teatinos
universities.desc: UMA's main science and technology campus — growing rapidly as Málaga becomes Spain's Silicon Coast, with a booming CS and data science department.

universities.name: ICADE – Universidad Pontificia Comillas
universities.desc: Comillas's acclaimed law and business faculty — ICADE E-1 (business + law double degree) is the most prestigious combined qualification in Spain's corporate world.

Madrid.desc: Spain's pulsating capital is where culture, politics, and youth collide. Three world-class universities, 50+ museums, and a nightlife scene that never stops make Madrid one of Europe's top student destinations.
highlights: Prado Museum — world's finest collection of Spanish painting
Retiro Park — 350-acre green lung in the city centre
Malasaña — bohemian student neighbourhood full of indie bars
Gran Vía — Madrid's Broadway: theatres, shops and buzz
Mercado de San Miguel — best tapas market in Spain
Madrid.studentLife: Madrid is home to over 200,000 students. The Erasmus community is one of the largest in Europe, accommodation costs €450–800/month for a room, and the city genuinely embraces young people.
Madrid.transport: 13-line Metro, EMT buses, and Cercanías suburban trains. A young person's monthly travel pass costs just €20 for under-26 — the cheapest in any European capital.
Madrid.nightlife: Malasaña for indie bars and live music, Huertas for cocktail lounges, Lavapiés for multicultural late-night spots. Clubs open at 2 AM and run until 7 AM.
pros: Best job market in all of Spain
Largest student community in the country
Cheapest monthly transit in any European capital
Museums, galleries & cultural events every weekend

Barcelona.desc: Europe's most visually stunning city offers Gaudí masterpieces, a working Mediterranean beach, and a startup ecosystem second only to London in Southern Europe. Barcelona is bilingual — Spanish and Catalan — and internationally minded.
highlights: Sagrada Família — Gaudí's unfinished 140-year masterpiece
Gothic Quarter — 2,000 years of layered history
Barceloneta Beach — the city's sandy backyard
Park Güell — Gaudí's mosaic hilltop garden
Palau de la Música — UNESCO-listed concert hall
Barcelona.studentLife: Barcelona has 7 universities and over 120,000 students. Gràcia and El Raval are the go-to student neighbourhoods. Many programmes are offered in English.
Barcelona.transport: TMB Metro (12 lines), buses, FGC trains and a Bicing bike-share. A T-Jove 3-month travel pass costs around €40.
Barcelona.nightlife: Barceloneta clubs right by the sea, Rambla del Poblenou for alternative bars, Eixample for rooftop cocktail terraces. Barcelona's nights end at 6 AM.
pros: Mediterranean beach 20 min from university
Strong English-taught degree programmes
Top EU hub for tech startups & design studios
Gaudí architecture is genuinely all around you

Valencia.desc: Valencia is Spain's underrated gem — 300 sunny days per year, an awe-inspiring City of Arts & Sciences, a booming tech scene, and a food culture built around the original paella. Significantly cheaper than Madrid or Barcelona.
highlights: City of Arts & Sciences — futuristic architecture complex
Turia Gardens — 9km of park built inside a former river bed
Mercado Central — one of Europe's most beautiful indoor markets
Malvarrosa Beach — 3km of sandy beach reachable by bike
Barrio del Carmen — bohemian historic quarter
Valencia.studentLife: Home to UPV and Universitat de València — combined student population of ~100,000. Russafa is Valencia's creative student area. Bikes are everywhere.
Valencia.transport: City bus network and exceptional cycling infrastructure. Valenbisi bike-share (€25/year). Monthly bus pass: €25. Valencia is the best cycling city in Spain.
Valencia.nightlife: Ruzafa neighbourhood for craft cocktail bars and indie concerts. Marina district for summer clubs. Quieter and more relaxed than Madrid.
pros: Most affordable of Spain's top student cities
Best cycling infrastructure in the country
300 sunny days and a proper beach
Home of paella, horchata, and Las Fallas festival

Sevilla.desc: Sevilla is Spain distilled into one city — flamenco, intricate Moorish architecture, Holy Week processions, and the deepest gastronomy culture in the country. Consistently ranked one of Europe's top Erasmus destinations.
highlights: Real Alcázar — breathtaking Moorish royal palace
La Giralda — iconic bell tower with panoramic city views
Barrio Santa Cruz — medieval Jewish quarter of winding alleys
Triana — the soul of flamenco, across the Guadalquivir river
Parque de María Luisa — romantic riverside park
Sevilla.studentLife: The University of Sevilla has 70,000+ students. Social life revolves around outdoor terraces and Friday tapas bars. Triana is the hub for Erasmus students. Rent: €300–500/month.
Sevilla.transport: Extensive bus network, a tram line, and excellent cycling paths along the river. Monthly travel pass: ~€35.
Sevilla.nightlife: La Alameda de Hércules for outdoor bars, Triana for authentic flamenco tablaos, Calle Betis along the Guadalquivir for magical summer evenings.
pros: Richest Moorish and Spanish heritage in Europe
Cheapest rent among Spain's major cities
Extraordinary food, flamenco, and festival culture
Strong Erasmus community and very welcoming locals

Granada.desc: Granada is a compact, passionate city where one of the world's greatest palaces — the Alhambra — watches over flamenco cave bars and a university that produces more students per capita than almost anywhere else in Spain. The free-tapas-with-every-drink culture is real.
highlights: Alhambra Palace & Generalife — UNESCO World Heritage
Albaicín district — Moorish hilltop neighbourhood
Free tapas bars — every drink comes with a free tapa. Always.
Sierra Nevada — Spain's top ski resort, 45 minutes by bus
Sacromonte caves — authentic flamenco in hillside venues
Granada.studentLife: The University of Granada has 55,000 students in a city of 232,000 — a 1:4 ratio. Life is affordable, social, and centred around plazas and tapas bars.
Granada.transport: Compact enough to walk or cycle almost everywhere. City buses cover the hillside neighbourhoods. Direct bus to the Sierra Nevada ski resort in winter.
Granada.nightlife: The Albaicín for cave flamenco venues. Calle Pedro Antonio for student bars. Calle Elvira for mixed crowds. The tapas culture means evenings turn into long social adventures.
pros: Cheapest city on this entire list
Highest student-to-resident ratio in Spain
Free tapas with every single drink
Ski resort 45 min away, beach 1h from the city

Bilbao.desc: Bilbao is Europe's greatest urban regeneration story. Once an industrial port city, today it's a world-class cultural destination. Expect serious rain, exceptional food (pintxos are a way of life), and a fierce, proud Basque identity.
highlights: Guggenheim Museum Bilbao — Frank Gehry's titanium masterpiece
Casco Viejo (Siete Calles) — the vibrant old town with pintxos bars
Mercado de la Ribera — Europe's largest covered food market
San Mamés Stadium — Athletic Club's iconic cathedral of football
Nervión Riverfront — the regenerated waterfront walkway
Bilbao.studentLife: The University of the Basque Country (EHU/UPV) is a major research university. Bilbao's compact size (everything is walkable) and low crime rate make it ideal for students.
Bilbao.transport: Efficient 2-line Metro, tram, and Bilbobus network. Most of the city is walkable. The Bilbao Card offers unlimited transport plus museum discounts.
Bilbao.nightlife: The Casco Viejo's Siete Calles for pintxos bar-hopping (txikiteo). Pozas street and Ledesma for late bars. A smaller club scene but rich live music culture.
pros: World-class architecture, design and food culture
Genuinely the best gastronomy in all of Spain
Very safe, compact and walkable for students
Strong research universities with international links

Salamanca.desc: Salamanca is the original university city. Founded in 1218, the University of Salamanca is one of the four oldest universities in the world. The entire city centre — built in golden sandstone — is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
highlights: University of Salamanca façade — intricate Plateresque stonework from 1529
Plaza Mayor — widely considered the most beautiful square in Spain
Catedral Nueva & Vieja — two cathedrals in one building complex
Casa de las Conchas — shell-covered 15th century palace
The Frog on the Façade — find it for good luck on your exams!
Salamanca.studentLife: 26,000 university students in a city of 143,000. Plaza Mayor is the social centre of everything. Famous as the top destination for learning Castilian Spanish.
Salamanca.transport: Small enough to walk everywhere — the old town is pedestrianised and compact. Direct trains to Madrid (1h 30min AVE high-speed) and Valladolid.
Salamanca.nightlife: Gran Vía de Salamanca for bars, Calle Prior for clubs, Plaza Mayor terraces in summer. The student population means the city has a lively nightlife far beyond its size.
pros: Cheapest city on the entire list by a significant margin
Most historic university in Spain (founded 1218)
Entire city centre is UNESCO World Heritage
Best place in the world to learn authentic Castilian Spanish

cityCards.desc: Spain's vibrant capital — world-class museums, buzzing nightlife, and the country's best job market.
Europe's most creative city — Gaudí architecture, beach life and a thriving startup scene.
Home of paella and sunshine — Valencia balances culture and coastal life at very affordable prices.
Flamenco, tapas and orange trees — Sevilla is Andalusia's beating heart and one of Spain's most beautiful cities.
A compact student city with the Alhambra on the hill — amazing culture, nightlife, and the cheapest tapas in Spain.
Transformed by the Guggenheim, Bilbao is a design-forward city with a rich culinary scene and proud Basque identity.
Home to one of Europe's oldest universities — Salamanca's golden sandstone old town is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
